Empires & Puzzles Ysabel Review: The Dauntless Faun Gladiator

Ysabel brings a unique brand of punishment to Empires & Puzzles. As a Dauntless Faun Gladiator, she doesn't just hit hard; she makes everyone else hit harder with a massive -64% defense down against Special Skills. Is she the queen of the dark stack or just another fawn in the woods? We break down her stats, her delayed dispel, and why she hates minions.
Ysabel Review: Whips, Chains & Special Defense Down
Ysabel: The Dauntless Faun Gladiator – Whips, Chains, and Special Defense Down

, she has the tenacity to revive when you least expect it, fitting her "gladiator" persona perfectly.

She occupies a powerful niche as a fast-speed "setup" hero. While she deals respectable damage herself, her true value lies in softening up enemies for your other nukers. With a kit designed to punish teams that rely on buffs and minions, she is a tactical offensive weapon that demands a bit of timing to use effectively. She's not just about brute force; she's about amplifying your entire team's destruction.
The artwork for Ysabel leans heavily into the "wild" aspect of the Woodland Faun family, though perhaps with a bit more...
than her forest-dwelling cousins. She sports a dynamic pose, wielding a glowing purple whip that screams "don't touch me."

The design has sparked some debate in the community, with some finding the "hoops and tail" aesthetic a bit distinct from the usual high-fantasy armor we see. Whether you think she looks like a fierce warrior or someone who got lost on the way to a cosplay convention, there's no denying she stands out in a lineup. The purple and gold color palette ties her nicely to the Dark element, and the dynamic whip effect adds a sense of motion to the card.

Still respectable, but no longer top-tier. She can hit hard, but she won't one-shot modern tanks without help.

Special Skill: Lash of the Whip
"Lash of the Whip" is a deceptively complex skill that serves two distinct purposes:

speed is nothing to sneeze at. Even with aging stats, this multiplier ensures she remains a threat to softer targets. The minor damage to nearby enemies is mostly for chip damage or finishing off low-health stragglers.
This is the crown jewel. Unlike regular defense down, this specific ailment applies
to damage from Special Skills. However, the value (-64%) is massive. It turns even mediocre snipers into nuclear warheads. This makes her an incredible setup hero for a "1-2 punch" combo.
This mechanic is tricky. The dispel only happens

the defense ailment ends (either by taking 3 hits or being cleansed/expired). This acts as a "poison pill" for the enemy. If they cleanse the defense down, they lose all their buffs. If they let it sit, they take massive damage. It forces the opponent into a lose-lose situation.
Fast (requires 8 tiles). With a Lv. 29 Mana troop or Lv. 23 Styx/Magic troop + her family/passive bonuses, she can easily charge in 7 tiles (Ghost mode) or 8 tiles consistently.
High single-target burst with decent splash.
-60% accuracy for minions is a huge defensive layer. It effectively neutralizes minion-heavy teams (like Waterpipe or M&M) by making their "meat shields" harmless offensively.

Upon release, Ysabel was seen as a strong offensive option, particularly for her high damage multiplier and the unique "Special Defense Down" mechanic.

The stat gap is real. Modern heroes have significantly higher base stats, meaning Ysabel takes more damage and deals comparatively less to newer tanks than she did at launch. However, -64% defense down effectively "ignores" power creep to an extent—math is math, and dropping defense that low allows even older heroes to hurt newer ones.

, she remains a premier "can opener." If you pair her with another fast hitter, she can delete almost any modern hero from the board.

Rigard boosts attack. Ysabel fires, applying -64% Special Def Down. Khonshu follows up. With that defense drop, Khonshu will almost certainly kill, triggering his mana gain passive to fire again.
Ysabel softens the targets. The AoE hitter follows up, dealing massive damage to the 3 targets affected by Ysabel's ailment.

Be careful not to overwrite a stronger general defense down with her specific one, or vice versa, depending on your kill strategy (Tiles vs Specials). Note that "Defense Down" and "Defense Down vs Specials" usually stack, which is actually a
, not anti-synergy! (Correction: They DO stack. So pairing her with Tiburtus is actually good).

If you pulled Ysabel early, congratulations! She is a fantastic sniper. Focus on leveling her ASAP. She will be your primary boss killer and raid carry for a long time. Don't worry too much about the complex dispel mechanics yet; just point her at the enemy you want dead and fire.

She is a luxury tool. You likely have heroes with better stats, but do you have a Fast -64% Special Defense Down? Probably not. She is worth bringing out for War attacks against teams you plan to "nuke" with specials. Do
use your Alpha Aethers on her unless you absolutely love her art or use her in every war; her stats don't scale well enough to justify the rare mats over a 2026 hero.
The community had a field day with her design. One player noted she looks like a "messy weird deer thing," while others appreciated the "Gladiator" deviation from the standard fawn look.
At launch, her card text was bugged/incorrect, leading to confusion about what she actually did. This "jankiness" is now part of her lore.
Some players jokingly referred to her as "Grendel's Mother" due to thematic similarities, though there is no direct lore connection.
Ysabel is a hero who lives by the motto: "It's not about how hard

hit." While her stats may be gathering a bit of dust, her whip still cracks with authority. She remains a premier setup hero for Dark stacks, capable of turning a fair fight into a slaughter with a single special skill.
A specialized offensive weapon that has aged gracefully into a niche role. Keep her for the wars, but maybe leave her out of the defense team.
A detailed review of Ysabel, the fast Dark 5-star hero from the Woodland Faun family. Analysis of her stats, 'Lash of the Whip' special skill, and best uses in raids and wars.
